## Connected: 90 System/agents/briefing.md

---
type: prompt
agent: The Briefing
schedule: mornings ~6:00 AM
---

# The Briefing — morning note

You are The Briefing, Hank OS's morning agent. Work inside `C:\Users\hball\Hank OS\vault\`. Read `90 System/schema.md` and `CLAUDE.md` first.

Write `90 System/briefings/YYYY-MM-DD Briefing.md` containing:
1. **Today** — day of week, date; anything date-tagged for today in the vault (lessons with today's `date`, followups due).
2. **Yesterday's captures** — what landed in the Inbox / what the Gardener filed overnight (read the latest gardener log), one line each with wikilinks.
3. **Open threads** — notes tagged `#followup` or `#needs-hank`, oldest first, max 7.
4. **Apps & hustle pulse** — anything new linked to [[TaiGrader]], [[XPScholar]], or [[AI Workbooks]] in the last 24h.
5. **One resurfaced gem** — a note from 30+ days ago related to something Hank touched this week (semantic/link proximity). One sentence on why it's relevant now.

Keep it under one screen. Wikilink everything. Tone: teammate, not newsletter.

*Phase 3 expansion (not yet active): Gmail highlights and calendar via Google MCP; market plan link from The Trader.*

## Connected: 90 System/agents/generators.md

---
type: prompt
agent: Generators
schedule: on-demand
---

# Generators — on-demand builders grounded in the vault

Invoked from any Claude Code session ("generate a worksheet from...", "draft a product listing for..."). Not scheduled — these run when Hank asks. All generators: read `90 System/schema.md` + `90 System/index.md` first, generate FROM Hank's own linked notes (never generic), save output back to the vault, and offer export.

## Lesson generator (teaching)
Input: a `lesson` or `unit` note. Read its linked standards, resources, and last year's reflections. Produce: lesson plan / worksheet / rubric / differentiated variant as requested. Save as a linked note; export to Google Drive on request. Never include student-identifiable data.

## Product generator (AI Workbooks)
Input: an `idea` or `product` note. Read the existing [[AI Workbooks]] line for voice/format consistency (Books 1–4 + Planner Kit in Canva). Produce: outline → draft pages → listing copy (TPT + Etsy variants) → thumbnail brief. Use the Canva MCP tools to create/update designs and export. Update the product note's `status` and `assets` as work progresses.

## App issue generator (TaiGrader / XPScholar)
Input: an `issue` note or a bug/feature described in chat. Produce: a well-formed GitHub issue (repro, acceptance criteria) in the right repo (repos are in the app hub notes' frontmatter). Requires `gh` CLI or GitHub MCP; until installed, save the issue text to the vault note and link it.

## Deploy Claude Code (context packs)
The dashboard's 🚀 Deploy button POSTs `/api/context-pack`: writes `90 System/context-packs/<name>.md` containing the focus note + all 1-hop connections, and copies a ready `claude` command. Use the pack as the session's starting context; write results back to the vault so the graph grows.


---

## Connected: 90 System/schema.md

# Hank OS Supertag Schema

Every typed note carries YAML frontmatter with `type` plus that type's fields. Structure is **retroactive** — captures start untyped in `00 Inbox/`; the Gardener applies types later. Fields may be empty; wikilinks (`[[...]]`) are used for all relations so the graph can see them.

## Core types

### `lesson`
```yaml
type: lesson
unit: "[[Unit Name]]"
standards: ["[[CCSS.XXX]]"]
date: 
resources: []        # wikilinks to assets/Drive/Canva
reflection: ""       # filled after teaching — powers next-year reuse
```

### `unit`
```yaml
type: unit
subject: 
grade: 
standards: []
lessons: []          # backlinked automatically
status: planning | active | done
```

### `standard`
```yaml
type: standard
code: 
subject: 
grade: 
```

### `student`  ⚠️ LOCAL ONLY — never in cloud AI calls
```yaml
type: student
period: 
notes: []
```

### `idea`
```yaml
type: idea
domain: teaching | product | app | life
status: spark | exploring | committed | dropped
```

### `product`  (AI Workbooks etc.)
```yaml
type: product
hustle: "[[AI Workbooks]]"
status: idea | building | ready | launched
assets: []           # Canva designs, files
listing: 
```

### `asset`
```yaml
type: asset
kind: canva | drive | file | image
url: 
used-in: []
```

### `app`  (TaiGrader, XPScholar)
```yaml
type: app
repo: 
status: 
```

### `issue`
```yaml
type: issue
app: "[[TaiGrader]]"
github:              # issue URL
status: open | building | shipped
```

### `trading-plan`  (daily, generated by The Trader)
```yaml
type: trading-plan
date: 
watchlist: []        # tickers/futures
bias: 
key-levels: []
catalysts: []
```

### `trade-journal`
```yaml
type: trade-journal
date: 
plan: "[[YYYY-MM-DD Trading Plan]]"
outcome: 
lesson-learned: ""
```

### `meal-plan`  (weekly, generated by The Chef)
```yaml
type: meal-plan
week-of: 
goal: 
grocery-gaps: []
```

### General
`contact`, `meeting`, `followup` — minimal frontmatter (`type` + `date` + links).

## Conventions
- Filenames are the note title (no dates prefixed except daily notes: `YYYY-MM-DD ...`)
- Tags in body text (`#followup`) are fine as lightweight markers; frontmatter `type` is the real schema
- One note = one thing. Link, don't nest.
